daily_brief: The dominant story today remains the US-Iran war, now in its fifth week, with President Trump delivering a primetime address claiming 'core strategic objectives are nearing completion' while promising two to three more weeks of intense strikes [BBC], [France 24], [Globe and Mail]. Hours after his speech, Israeli air defenses intercepted multiple waves of Iranian missiles, underscoring the conflict's continued intensity [France 24], [The Hindu]. Meanwhile, NASA achieved a historic milestone as Artemis II successfully launched four astronauts on humanity's first crewed lunar voyage in over 50 years [BBC], [RFI], [Al Jazeera]. In the Pacific, a magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck off Indonesia's Ternate island, killing at least one person and triggering brief tsunami warnings across the region [BBC]. European attention turns to Hungary, where challenger Peter Magyar poses the most serious electoral threat to Viktor Orbán's 15-year rule ahead of April 12 elections [BBC Europe]. French authorities charged four individuals, including three minors, over a foiled bomb plot targeting Bank of America's Paris headquarters, with investigators exploring possible links to pro-Iran groups [France 24].

top_stories.headline: Trump Vows Two to Three More Weeks of 'Extremely Hard' Strikes on Iran But Offers No Exit Strategy
bullets: President Trump delivered his first primetime national address on the Iran war, claiming US-Israeli military objectives are 'nearing completion' after 32 days of conflict
Trump threatened to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' and destroy its electrical infrastructure if Tehran does not agree to a deal
The speech offered contradictory signals, suggesting the US might walk away from the conflict and leave other nations to resolve the Strait of Hormuz blockade
Polls show a consistent majority of Americans disapprove of the military operation launched on February 28

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized unanswered questions and lack of clear exit strategy

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted contradictions between winding down rhetoric and continued escalation

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on contradictory messages and mounting global economic crisis

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Quoted critics calling speech 'embarrassing and incoherent'; noted lack of new substance

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Straightforward factual coverage of speech and Iranian response

top_stories.headline: Israeli Air Defenses Intercept Multiple Iranian Missile Waves Hours After Trump Address
bullets: Israeli military reported intercepting three waves of Iranian missile fire early Thursday, including at least two attacks after Trump's speech
Several people were lightly wounded in the Tel Aviv area; widespread sirens triggered across the country
Iran's military vowed 'crushing' attacks against the US and Israel in response to Trump's threats
US embassy in Baghdad warned of potential attacks by pro-Iran militia groups within 24-48 hours

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Provided comprehensive liveblog coverage with regional context

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Factual reporting on military exchanges and oil price impacts

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on Israel's intensified attacks on Lebanon alongside Iran developments

top_stories.headline: NASA's Artemis II Launches Four Astronauts on First Crewed Lunar Mission in Over 50 Years
bullets: The 32-story Space Launch System rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center at 6:35 PM ET, carrying three American astronauts and one Canadian
The 10-day mission will orbit the Moon without landing, traveling further from Earth than any humans before
Approximately 400,000 people gathered to witness the launch, reminiscent of Apollo-era crowds
The mission is a key step toward NASA's goal of landing astronauts on the Moon's south pole by 2028, ahead of China's planned 2030 crewed lunar mission

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized historic nature and crew's journey

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted diverse crew composition and race with China

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Focused on US-China space competition context

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed timeline and technical mission details

top_stories.headline: Magnitude 7.4 Earthquake Strikes Off Indonesia, Killing One and Triggering Brief Tsunami Warnings
bullets: The earthquake struck in the Molucca Sea off Ternate island at 06:48 local time at a depth of 35km
At least one person died after being crushed by debris in North Sulawesi; another was injured jumping from a building
Tsunami warnings were issued for Guam, Japan, Malaysia, Papua New Guinea, Philippines, and Taiwan but lifted after two hours
Residents described it as the strongest earthquake felt in the region in at least six years

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Included eyewitness accounts and regional context

top_stories.headline: France Charges Four Over Foiled Bomb Plot at Bank of America Paris Headquarters
bullets: French authorities charged a man in his early twenties and three minors with 'terrorist criminal conspiracy' over an attempted attack near the Champs-Élysées
The plot, thwarted before dawn on Saturday, allegedly involved placing a homemade explosive device outside the US bank
Investigators suspect the adult recruited minors Thursday-Friday night, offering 500-1,000 euros for the job
Anti-terror prosecutors are exploring possible links to a little-known Islamist group with suspected ties to Iran, though no firm connection established

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage noting timing one month after US-Israeli strikes on Iran

additional_stories.headline: Peter Magyar emerges as biggest threat to Viktor Orbán's 15-year rule ahead of Hungary's April 12 elections
Two migrants die attempting English Channel crossing near Calais as UK-France extend border deal talks
US Supreme Court justices signal skepticism toward Trump's executive order restricting birthright citizenship
Italy fails to qualify for third consecutive World Cup after penalty shootout loss to Bosnia-Herzegovina
Macron visits South Korea for state talks on trade, AI, nuclear energy and security cooperation
WhatsApp says Italian surveillance company ASIGINT tricked around 200 users into downloading spyware
Human Rights Watch reports Burkina Faso army and jihadists killed more than 1,800 civilians since 2023
Barrick Mining delays Pakistan's largest copper-gold mine project by one year citing Iran war security risks
Indonesia and Australia warn of punishments against Meta and Google for failing to enforce under-16 social media bans
Trial begins for 36 defendants in Greece's deadliest train disaster that killed 57 people in 2023
